2026-04-23T02:51:35.4495564Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4496006Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4496340Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4496648Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4496892Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4497086Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4497217Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4497373Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4497515Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4497704Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T02:51:35.4498098Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:51:35.4498306Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. 2026-04-23T02:54:24.5162546Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T02:52:33.029635532Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58540,\"title\":\"[AgentServer Responses] Docs: Document persistence failure resilience and request-correlation headers\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58526 (commit ab77838) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58526 released `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.4 with these new features in `BinaryDataExpansionHelpers.cs` and the server middleware:\\n\\n- **Persistence failure resilience** — storage failures no longer crash or stall responses; they now complete gracefully with `status: \\u0026#34;failed\\u0026#34;` and `error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;`. Covers streaming, background+streaming, background+non-streaming, and synchronous modes.\\n- **`x-request-id` response header** — all endpoints now return this header for request correlation; its value is also injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` on error responses.\\n- **`traceparent` header in Foundry storage logging** — W3C distributed trace ID is now included in every log entry from `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, enabling correlation with backend traces.\\n- **`GetInputExpanded` content normalization** — string shorthand on `ItemMessage.Content` is now auto-expanded to the canonical array form.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section in `README.md` does not document the `storage_error` error code or explain what `status: \\u0026#34;failed\\u0026#34;` means when a storage operation fails, leaving operators without guidance on how to detect and respond to these failures.\\n- The **Logging** section in `README.md` mentions OpenTelemetry activity source but does not mention the `traceparent` header propagation in Foundry storage logging or how to correlate SDK log entries with backend distributed traces.\\n- There is **no documentation** anywhere in the README or samples about the `x-request-id` response header or how callers can use `error.additionalInfo.request_id` for client-side error correlation.\\n- The **input normalization behavior** (string shorthand → canonical array) changed in beta.4; this implicit contract is not documented in the README\\u0026#39;s `ResponseContext` / `GetInputItemsAsync` sections, which could confuse implementors relying on the raw `Content` shape.\\n- There is **no sample** demonstrating error handling for storage failures or how to extract correlation IDs from error responses.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\n**1a. Update the Troubleshooting → Common errors section**\\n\\nLocate the \\u0026#34;Common errors\\u0026#34; subsection and append the following bullet after the existing entries:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Storage failure (`error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;`)**: A backend storage operation failed after the\\n  response began executing. The response is returned with `status: \\u0026#34;failed\\u0026#34;` and\\n  `error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;` rather than leaving it permanently stuck at `in_progress`. For\\n  streaming responses, the terminal `response.failed` SSE event is emitted automatically. For\\n  synchronous responses, an HTTP 500 is returned with the storage error details.\\n  Check the `x-request-id` response header (or `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the error body)\\n  to correlate the failure with backend logs.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand the Logging subsection**\\n\\nLocate the Logging subsection (currently: \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source...\\u0026#34;) and expand it to:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nEnable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\\n\\n#### Request correlation\\n\\nEvery response from the server includes an `x-request-id` response header. The value is resolved\\nfrom the OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → a generated GUID. On error responses,\\nthe same value is injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the response body, enabling\\nclient-side correlation without needing access to server logs.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage logging\\n\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level,\\nincluding the HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers\\n(`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Each log entry also includes the\\n`traceparent` header (W3C Trace Context format), enabling direct correlation between SDK log\\nentries and backend distributed traces in any W3C-compatible tracing system (Application Insights,\\nJaeger, Zipkin, etc.).\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Update ResponseContext documentation (Key Concepts section)**\\n\\nIn the `ResponseContext` bullet list (after the `RawBody` entry), add a note about `GetInputItemsAsync` normalizing string shorthand:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Content normalization**: When `input` contains `ItemMessage` entries whose `content` is a\\n  plain JSON string (e.g., `\\u0026#34;Hello\\u0026#34;`), the library automatically expands it to the canonical array\\n  form (`[{\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;input_text\\u0026#34;,\\u0026#34;text\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;Hello\\u0026#34;}]`). `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync`\\n  always return normalized content regardless of how the client submitted the request.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for the prose-only additions above. However, if a sample for error handling is desired:\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleErrorHandling.cs`\\n   - Demonstrate reading `x-request-id` from an error response and logging `error.code`\\n   - Wrap the relevant code with `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ErrorCorrelation` / `#endregion`\\n\\n2. **Add placeholder in README** in the Troubleshooting section:\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ⚠️ **Troubleshooting** — missing `storage_error` and correlation header documentation (fix in Step 1a and 1b above)\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for doc-only edits; run to verify\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; Update the Troubleshooting → Common errors section**\\n\\nLocate the \\u0026#34;Common errors\\u0026#34; subsection and append the following bullet after the existing entries:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Storage failure (`error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;`)**: A backend storage operation failed after the\\n  response began executing. For\\n  streaming responses, the terminal `response.failed` SSE event is emitted automatically. For\\n  synchronous responses, an HTTP 500 is returned with the storage error details.\\n  Check the `x-request-id` response header (or `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the error body)\\n  to correlate the failure with backend logs.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand the Logging subsection**\\n\\nLocate the Logging subsection (currently: \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source...\\u0026#34;) and expand it to:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nEnable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\\n\\n#### Request correlation\\n\\nEvery response from the server includes an `x-request-id` response header. The value is resolved\\nfrom the OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → a generated GUID. On error responses,\\nthe same value is injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the response body, enabling\\nclient-side correlation without needing access to server logs.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage logging\\n\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level,\\nincluding the HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers\\n(`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Each log entry also includes the\\n`traceparent` header (W3C Trace Context format), enabling direct correlation between SDK log\\nentries and backend distributed traces in any W3C-compatible tracing system (Application Insights,\\nJaeger, Zipkin, etc.).\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Update ResponseContext documentation (Key Concepts section)**\\n\\nIn the `ResponseContext` bullet list (after the `RawBody` entry), add a note about `GetInputItemsAsync` normalizing string shorthand:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Content normalization**: When `input` contains `ItemMessage` entries whose `content` is a\\n  plain JSON string (e.g., `\\u0026#34;Hello\\u0026#34;`), the library automatically expands it to the canonical array\\n  form (`[{\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;input_text\\u0026#34;,\\u0026#34;text\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;Hello\\u0026#34;}]`). `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync`\\n  always return normalized content regardless of how the client submitted the request.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for the prose-only additions above. However, if a sample for error handling is desired:\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleErrorHandling.cs`\\n   - Demonstrate reading `x-request-id` from an error response and logging `error.code`\\n   - Wrap the relevant code with `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ErrorCorrelation` / `#endregion`\\n\\n2. **Add placeholder in README** in the Troubleshooting section:\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ⚠️ **Troubleshooting** — missing `storage_error` and correlation header documentation (fix in Step 1a and 1b above)\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for doc-only edits; run to verify\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-23T02:54:24.5278447Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T02:52:42.972134355Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58540,\"title\":\"[AgentServer Responses] Docs: Document persistence failure resilience and request-correlation headers\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58526 (commit ab77838) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58526 released `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.4 with these new features in `BinaryDataExpansionHelpers.cs` and the server middleware:\\n\\n- **Persistence failure resilience** — storage failures no longer crash or stall responses; they now complete gracefully with `status: \\u0026#34;failed\\u0026#34;` and `error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;`. Covers streaming, background+streaming, background+non-streaming, and synchronous modes.\\n- **`x-request-id` response header** — all endpoints now return this header for request correlation; its value is also injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` on error responses.\\n- **`traceparent` header in Foundry storage logging** — W3C distributed trace ID is now included in every log entry from `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, enabling correlation with backend traces.\\n- **`GetInputExpanded` content normalization** — string shorthand on `ItemMessage.Content` is now auto-expanded to the canonical array form.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section in `README.md` does not document the `storage_error` error code or explain what `status: \\u0026#34;failed\\u0026#34;` means when a storage operation fails, leaving operators without guidance on how to detect and respond to these failures.\\n- The **Logging** section in `README.md` mentions OpenTelemetry activity source but does not mention the `traceparent` header propagation in Foundry storage logging or how to correlate SDK log entries with backend distributed traces.\\n- There is **no documentation** anywhere in the README or samples about the `x-request-id` response header or how callers can use `error.additionalInfo.request_id` for client-side error correlation.\\n- The **input normalization behavior** (string shorthand → canonical array) changed in beta.4; this implicit contract is not documented in the README\\u0026#39;s `ResponseContext` / `GetInputItemsAsync` sections, which could confuse implementors relying on the raw `Content` shape.\\n- There is **no sample** demonstrating error handling for storage failures or how to extract correlation IDs from error responses.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\n**1a. Update the Troubleshooting → Common errors section**\\n\\nLocate the \\u0026#34;Common errors\\u0026#34; subsection and append the following bullet after the existing entries:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Storage failure (`error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;`)**: A backend storage operation failed after the\\n  response began executing. The response is returned with `status: \\u0026#34;failed\\u0026#34;` and\\n  `error.code: \\u0026#34;storage_error\\u0026#34;` rather than leaving it permanently stuck at `in_progress`. For\\n  streaming responses, the terminal `response.failed` SSE event is emitted automatically. For\\n  synchronous responses, an HTTP 500 is returned with the storage error details.\\n  Check the `x-request-id` response header (or `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the error body)\\n  to correlate the failure with backend logs.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand the Logging subsection**\\n\\nLocate the Logging subsection (currently: \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source...\\u0026#34;) and expand it to:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nEnable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\\n\\n#### Request correlation\\n\\nEvery response from the server includes an `x-request-id` response header. The value is resolved\\nfrom the OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → a generated GUID. On error responses,\\nthe same value is injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the response body, enabling\\nclient-side correlation without needing access to server logs.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage logging\\n\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level,\\nincluding the HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers\\n(`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Each log entry also includes the\\n`traceparent` header (W3C Trace Context format), enabling direct correlation between SDK log\\nentries and backend distributed traces in any W3C-compatible tracing system (Application Insights,\\nJaeger, Zipkin, etc.).\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Update ResponseContext documentation (Key Concepts section)**\\n\\nIn the `ResponseContext` bullet list (after the `RawBody` entry), add a note about `GetInputItemsAsync` normalizing string shorthand:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Content normalization**: When `input` contains `ItemMessage` entries whose `content` is a\\n  plain JSON string (e.g., `\\u0026#34;Hello\\u0026#34;`), the library automatically expands it to the canonical array\\n  form (`[{\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;input_text\\u0026#34;,\\u0026#34;text\\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34;Hello\\u0026#34;}]`). `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync`\\n  always return normalized content regardless of how the client submitted the request.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for the prose-only additions above. However, if a sample for error handling is desired:\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleErrorHandling.cs`\\n   - Demonstrate reading `x-request-id` from an error response and logging `error.code`\\n   - Wrap the relevant code with `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ErrorCorrelation` / `#endregion`\\n\\n2. **Add placeholder in README** in the Troubleshooting section:\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ⚠️ **Troubleshooting** — missing `storage_error` and correlation header documentation (fix in Step 1a and 1b above)\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; The equivalent manual setup (`AddInvocationsServer()` + `MapInvocationsServer()`) is missing.\\n- `InvocationsServerOptions` is part of the public API but is never mentioned or described in the README.\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes desc
